batches.title: Monday Lunch Components: Ground Beef, Lentils, Roasted Sweet Potato & Peppers
steps: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C.
2. Dice 300 g sweet potato into 2 cm cubes. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp smoked paprika, salt and pepper. Spread on a baking tray lined with parchment.
3. Slice 3 bell peppers (mixed colours) into strips. Toss with 1 tsp olive oil, salt. Spread on a second baking tray.
4. Place both trays in oven: sweet potato for 25 min, peppers for 20 min. Check and turn halfway. Remove when tender and lightly caramelised. Cool completely.

5. Rinse 200 g dried green lentils. Place in a medium pot with 600 ml cold water and 1/2 tsp salt. Bring to boil, reduce to simmer 20 min until just tender. Drain and season with 1/2 tsp cumin, black pepper. Cool completely.

6. Heat 1 tsp olive oil in a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Add 600 g lean ground beef, breaking up with a spoon. Cook 7–8 min until fully browned. Season with 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp garlic powder, salt. Cook 2 more min. Cool completely.
7. Portion into airtight containers: beef separate from lentils and roasted veg. Label Monday. Refrigerate immediately.
yields: 600 g cooked seasoned ground beef (≈3 portions)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mexican Beef & Lentil Bowl: reheat 200 g beef + 120 g lentils + 120 g sweet potato + 100 g peppers in microwave; assemble bowl with lentils first, top with beef; squeeze lime.

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Tuesday Lunch Components: Roasted Eggplant, Roasted Chickpeas & Quinoa
steps: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C (can use same oven session as batch 1).
2. Cube 1 large eggplant (≈400 g) into 2 cm pieces. Toss with 1.5 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp dried oregano, salt. Spread on a lined baking tray.
3. Drain and rinse 2 cans (800 g) chickpeas. Pat dry with paper towel — this ensures crispiness. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp turmeric, salt. Spread on a separate tray.
4. Roast eggplant 25 min and chickpeas 20 min, turning once at the halfway mark. Both should be golden. Cool completely.

5. Rinse 300 g quinoa in a fine sieve until water runs clear. Place in a medium pot with 600 ml water and 1/2 tsp salt. Bring to boil, reduce to low, cover and cook 15 min. Remove from heat, leave covered 5 min. Fluff with a fork. Cool completely.
6. Store quinoa, eggplant and chickpeas in separate labelled airtight containers. Refrigerate immediately.

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch — Mediterranean Baked Salmon with Roasted Eggplant, Chickpeas & Quinoa: bake fresh salmon 14 min while reheating 130 g quinoa + 100 g eggplant + 80 g chickpeas; drizzle tahini-lemon sauce.
day_of_assembly.Friday: Lunch — Asian Sesame Beef Bowl with Broccoli, Carrots & Quinoa: reheat 130 g quinoa (from mid-week Wednesday fresh cook); stir-fry fresh beef strips and vegetables; serve over quinoa.

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.

batches.title: Wednesday Lunch Components: French Beef & White Bean Skillet with Roasted Zucchini
steps: 1. Preheat oven to 200°C.

2. Slice 2 large zucchini into 0.5 cm rounds. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp dried oregano, 1/2 tsp black pepper, salt. Spread on a lined baking tray. Roast 20 min until golden and slightly charred at edges. Cool and refrigerate.
3. Mince 3 garlic cloves. Heat 1.5 tbsp olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Add 500 g lean beef chunks; sear undisturbed 2–3 min per side until browned all over.
4. Add garlic, 1 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp dried thyme, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp black pepper; stir 1 min.
5. Add 2 cans (800 g total) drained white beans and 1 can (400 g) diced tomatoes. Stir well. Bring to a simmer; cook covered on low heat 20 min until beef is tender. Taste and adjust salt.
6. Cool completely. Portion into airtight containers. Refrigerate immediately — MUST be eaten by Wednesday (day 3 beef rule).
yields: ≈1.1 kg beef & white bean skillet (≈4 portions household)

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Lunch — French-Style Beef & White Bean Skillet with Roasted Zucchini & Sourdough: reheat 300 g skillet in pan 5–6 min; microwave 120 g zucchini; plate zucchini first, then skillet, sourdough on side.

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Wednesday Mid-Week Fresh Quinoa Cook (for Friday Lunch)
steps: 1. NOTE: This batch is NOT cooked on Sunday — cook it on Wednesday evening after dinner (takes only 5 min active). This ensures it is within the 3-day safety window for Friday.
2. On Wednesday evening: rinse 300 g quinoa in a fine sieve. Place in a pot with 600 ml water and 1/2 tsp salt. Bring to boil, cover, simmer 15 min. Fluff and cool uncovered 10 min.
3. Transfer to an airtight container, label 'Friday Quinoa', refrigerate immediately. Use by Friday (2 days).

day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (grains pasta is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 5).

storage.safety_note: Add a splash of water before reheating to restore moisture. Use within 3 days.
